title: Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
bank_name: Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt?
faq.answer: Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (ABK-Egypt) is a retail and commercial bank operating in Egypt as part of the broader Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait Group. The bank provides comprehensive financial solutions to individuals and businesses throughout Egypt, offering a wide range of Sharia-compliant and conventional banking products. ABK-Egypt operates 46 branches across Egypt and is recognized as one of the country's significant private banking institutions.

faq.question: When was Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt founded?
faq.answer: The bank has roots dating back to 1978 when it was originally established as Alexandria Kuwait International Bank (AKIB), one of Egypt's original private banks. The institution later became known as Egyptian Commercial Bank. In 2005, Piraeus Bank Group acquired the bank and renamed it Piraeus Bank Egypt. In November 2015, Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait completed the acquisition of Piraeus Bank Egypt to expand its regional presence. The commercial register was officially amended on July 25, 2016, changing the bank's name to "Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait – EGYPT (SAE)."

faq.question: Where is Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt is headquartered at its Smart Village location in Egypt. The bank maintains 46 branches strategically located throughout the country to serve both retail and corporate customers.

faq.question: Where does Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt operate?
faq.answer: ABK-Egypt operates exclusively within Egypt through its network of 46 branches distributed across major cities and commercial centers throughout the country. The bank's extensive branch network ensures accessibility for customers in urban and suburban areas across Egypt.

faq.question: What services does Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt offer?
faq.answer: ABK-Egypt offers comprehensive banking services including personal savings and current accounts, loans, credit cards, and digital banking solutions. Corporate services include business financing, trade finance, cash management, and treasury services. The bank provides online banking and a mobile app for 24/7 account access, bill payments, and money transfers. ABK-Egypt offers international banking services including wire transfers, online transfers, and mobile transfers to over 200 countries and territories. The bank provides competitive foreign exchange services and foreign currency accounts in US dollars, Euros, and British pounds. Corporate clients benefit from credit planning, business planning, investment planning, asset management, and wealth protection services.

faq.question: What makes Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt unique?
faq.answer: ABK-Egypt distinguishes itself through its commitment to both technological innovation and corporate social responsibility. The bank has invested significantly in digital capabilities and technology integration, enabling seamless customer experiences through its online banking platform and mobile app. ABK-Egypt actively engages in corporate social responsibility initiatives, including blood drive campaigns supporting hospitals and community health initiatives. In 2025, the bank announced a 32% increase in net profit to EGP 3.9 billion, demonstrating strong financial performance. The bank sponsored the 16th edition of the Kuwaiti Business Week in Egypt, reinforcing economic cooperation between Kuwait and Egypt. ABK-Egypt benefits from its parent group's strong regional presence and expertise while maintaining local market knowledge and specialized services for Egyptian customers.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt?
faq.answer: Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait - Egypt is a retail and commercial bank operating as a joint-stock company under Egyptian banking regulations. The bank is licensed and supervised by the Central Bank of Egypt and functions as a private banking institution serving both individual and corporate clients across various economic sectors.
